---
title: "About"
---

::::: about-container
::: about-image
<img src="images/profile_rijkslib.jpg" alt="Fuminari Miura"/>
:::

::: about-text
I am a senior researcher at the Dutch National Institute for Public Health and the Environment [(RIVM)](https://www.rivm.nl/f-fuminari-miura-phd). I also have an academic appointment at Nagasaki University, Japan.

My work focuses on understanding how transmission processes unfold across heterogeneous populations and how epidemic theory can inform practical control strategies.

My recent research particularly interested in:

-   Real-time outbreak analytics\
-   Designing targeted control strategies\
-   Epidemic theory for decision making\
-   Dimensional compression and interpretability\

<!--## Research focus
to be updated
- Real-time outbreak analytics
- Designing targeted control strategies
- Epidemic theory for decision making
- Low dimensional representation of dynamical systemsI aim to bridge epidemic theory and decision making

-   network and multi-layer epidemic models\
-   time-varying dominance of transmission routes\
-   reduced-order representations of complex epidemic dynamics
through parsimonious, interpretable models. You can find my CV [here](cv.html).
 -->
 
:::
:::::
